title: Criticality and Quench Dynamics at the Anderson Transition of a Chern Insulator
authors: Lara Ulčakar, Jernej Mravlje, Gal Lemut, Tomaž Rejec
abstract: We study the critical properties of the topological Anderson phase transition in a strongly disordered Chern insulator, separating the topological phase from a trivial Anderson insulator. We show that the transition is characterized by a non-zero electrical conductance and by the emergence of a critical length scale in the real-space profile of the local Chern marker. From this, we extract the correlation-length and the dynamical critical exponents, which are consistent with those of non-interacting models of the integer quantum Hall effect. We then ramp the disorder strength across the transition and study the ensuing dynamics. In contrast to clean topological systems, we find that the excitation density does not follow the Kibble-Zurek scaling. The non-equilibrium length scale associated with the local Chern marker is decoupled from the generation of excitations. For studied system sizes, we find it to be close to the Kibble-Zurek prediction for the topological-to-trivial quench, while it deviates from it for the reverse direction.
summary: This work analyzes the topological Anderson transition in a disordered Chern insulator, finding critical exponents consistent with the Integer Quantum Hall Effect. Crucially, the study examines non-equilibrium dynamics during disorder quenches, revealing that the excitation generation and the associated length scale deviate from standard Kibble-Zurek scaling, depending on the quench direction.
main_problem: The study investigates the critical properties and non-equilibrium dynamics across the topological Anderson phase transition in a strongly disordered Chern insulator.
main_result: The transition is characterized by critical exponents consistent with the Integer Quantum Hall Effect, but the non-equilibrium excitation density does not follow standard Kibble-Zurek scaling, showing direction-dependent behavior.
method: The authors employ finite-size scaling, analysis of the Local Chern Marker (LCM) profile, and quench dynamics simulations to extract critical exponents and non-equilibrium scaling laws.
model_or_system: The system is a disordered Chern insulator modeled by the half-filled disordered Qi-Wu-Zhang (QWZ) model, which exhibits a transition between a topological and a trivial Anderson insulating phase.
key_observables:
Chern number, electrical conductance, Local Chern Marker (LCM) profile, correlation length ($\xi$), excitation density, and critical exponents ($
u, z$).
important_parameters: Disorder strength ($W$), system size ($N$), and quench time ($	au$).
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on comparing results to established universality classes (IQHE) and the Kibble-Zurek mechanism, while acknowledging potential limitations regarding convergence in the thermodynamic limit.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the phase diagram (Chern number vs. conductance vs. disorder), finite-size scaling plots for critical observables, and real-space profiles of the LCM at different disorder strengths.
paper_structure: The paper first establishes the critical nature of the transition using ground-state properties (conductance, Chern number) and extracts critical exponents. It then moves to non-equilibrium dynamics, studying the quench evolution of excitation density and the characteristic length scale, comparing these findings to the Kibble-Zurek prediction.
why_interesting: The study bridges topological physics, Anderson localization, and non-equilibrium dynamics, providing insights into how topological protection breaks down under disorder and quench protocols, which is relevant for understanding quantum phase transitions in engineered materials.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Phase diagram in the (u, W) parameter space of the (a) Chern number and (b) the electrical conduc- tance. (c) The energy level-spacing variance of the states per energy window of width 0.1, at u = −1. Data is averaged over (a) 10, (b) 50 and (c) 100 disorder realizations. Dashed line indicates the values of parameters during the quench.

FIG. 2. Finite-size scaling plots across the phase transition of (a) the Chern number, (b) the electrical conductance and (c) the correlation length ξ/N estimated from the LCM profile. Quantities are presented as functions of the rescaled control parameter (W −Wc)N 1/ν for different system sizes.
FIG. 3. Real-space profile of the local Chern marker of the ground state at different disorder strengths that range across the phase transition.
FIG. 4. (a) AC conductivity σxx as a function of disorder strength at various ω. (b) Full width at half maximum of σxx as function of the rescaled frequency ωN z for different system sizes.

FIG. 5. Excitation density (a), (d) during quenches with different τ and (b), (e) at the end of the quench as a function of τ. (c), (f) show spatial distribution of excitations after a quench with τ = 10 and N = 100. (a), (b) and (c) correspond to the trivial-to-topological protocol and (d), (e) and (f) to the topological-to-trivial protocol.
